basic_details.job_title: NIT Calicut Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.short_information: National Institute of Technology Calicut (NIT Calicut) has officially released a notification for the recruitment of 1 Junior Research Fellow Posts. This recruitment drive offers a great opportunity for candidates looking for government jobs. Interested and eligible candidates can apply by sending their resume and documents to the official email address.

age_limit.details: Please refer to the official notification for age limit details.

qualification.education_qualification: MTech (Machine Design, Manufacturing Technology, Materials Science and Technology, Nanotechnology/Materials Science) with GATE/NET qualified, MSc (Chemistry, Physics, Nanoscience) with GATE/NET qualified (6.0 CGPA/60%)
qualification.details: Junior Research Fellow: Essential: MTech in Machine Design, Manufacturing Technology, Materials Science and Technology, Nanotechnology /Materials Science or equivalent (candidates who have completed the course or are in the final year are eligible) with GATE/NET qualified (or) MSc Chemistry/ Physics /Nanoscience or equivalent. (6.0 CGPA/60%) with Gate/NET qualified. Desirable: Candidate with knowledge of high-temperature deformation of materials and materials characterization, including SEM and EBSD. The candidate should have strong programming skills, preferably in MATLAB® or Python.

how_to_apply: Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Prepare your application with complete bio-data including personal details, educational qualifications, and experience.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs.
Clearly mention the post applied for in the subject line of the email.
Send the complete application to the official email address mentioned in the notification.
Ensure the email is sent before the last date: 11-05-2026.
Keep a copy of the sent email for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the NIT Calicut Junior Research Fellow recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your application via email is 11th May 2026. Ensure your application reaches the concerned authority before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the Junior Research Fellow position?
faq.answer: Candidates need to have an MTech in specific disciplines with GATE/NET qualification or an MSc in Chemistry, Physics, or Nanoscience with GATE/NET qualification and a minimum CGPA of 6.0 or 60%.

faq.question: Q3. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary offered for the Junior Research Fellow post?
faq.answer: The selected candidate will receive a monthly stipend of Rs. 37,000/- plus 20% House Rent Allowance (HRA).

faq.question: Q5. How will candidates be selected for the Junior Research Fellow position?
faq.answer: The selection process involves shortlisting of candidates, followed by a written test and/or an online interview.

faq.question: Q6. What is the total number of vacancies available?
faq.answer: There is one vacancy for the post of Junior Research Fellow announced by NIT Calicut.

faq.question: Q7. What is the mode of application for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Interested candidates are required to apply through email by sending their resume and required documents.

faq.question: Q8. Where is NIT Calicut located?
faq.answer: National Institute of Technology Calicut is located in Calicut, Kerala.

faq.question: Q9. Are candidates in their final year eligible to apply?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates who are in their final year of MTech are also eligible to apply for this position.

faq.question: Q10. What are the desirable qualifications for the Junior Research Fellow role?
faq.answer: Desirable qualifications include knowledge of high-temperature deformation of materials, materials characterization (SEM, EBSD), and strong programming skills in MATLAB® or Python.
